title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers reader outcomes, repository baseline, static architecture, and evidence into a changed reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter with automated and judgment evidence…
objectives: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Reader Outcome
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 3612 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Reader Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Reader Outcome, keep Reader Outcome connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

For commit to the mission model, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Reader Outcome for reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter.
State the Reader Outcome preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 5231 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-evidence-ladder-gapw3w" that uses case 8370 to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-evidence-ladder-gapw3w
# Competency: ssg-evidence-ladder.
# Case 8370: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_evidence_ladder_gapw3w(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_gapw3w = "gapw3w-w3wpag-8370"
    assert evidence_variant_gapw3w
    evidence_axes_gapw3w = (
        "readergapw3w",
        "source8370",
        "parsew3wpag",
        "routegapw3w8370",
        "artifact8370gapw3w",
        "recoveryw3wpag8370",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_gapw3w)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
